item.content:encoded: https://www.reddit.com/r/SCP/comments/io0bka/my_design_for_scp2786/?utm_source=share&utm_medium=web2x&context=3

item.title: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded: First image has been removed due to the wiki no longer permitting images licensed under any Non-Derivative Creative Commons licenses.

item.title: SCP-2786 and 245 connection
item.content:encoded: Hmm this is pretty similar to SCP-245. Could they be connected or even be the same entity?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great one.
It's right u know. About the foundation. Giving people hope just to destroy them later.
That is cruel. "Foundation is cold, not cruel." If someone needs to die, let the D-class die, right?
But what about all these D-class people who died in vain?
There are experiments which were completely unnecessary.
Foundation could just neutralize most of the dangerous scp's and be done with it. But instead they keep sending people to their horrible deaths just to satisfy their curiosity.
Hell, some of them don't even deserve to die. Some are just young men/women trying to survive.
I read a couple scp's where 18-25 year olds were sacrificed unnecessarily.
Scp Foundation is not a meat grinder. Peoples lives are not toys, even if they were horrible people. out of all, people who has to risk their own lives every day, has to know.

Never thought I'd write something this popular. To celebrate, I've added a couple new complaint pictures and made some tonal adjustments, added a secret or two. In fact, several of my articles have been updated, most notably SCP-3311 and Your Future is Bright. More to come as my schedule permits

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed, with a new one

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Images are broken

item.title: My favorite SCP ever
item.content:encoded: I remeber getting off of work after a long day and i had a 20 minute drive home. I decided to play the Volgun when I saw he put up a new video SCP-2786. I was in awe. I had to literally stop my car to hear the rest. It was so amazing. I felt sad for SCP-2786 yet I knew he needed to be contained. And I havent stopped listening to it since then. Great job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They weren't doing anything wrong, right? All they were really doing is giving movies, games, and books a better ending, I honestly feel bad for the guy..

in this moment1, I am speechless.

this is beyond my expectations, thanks for letting me be a part of this community.

Footnotes
1. 02:08 PST 10/13/2018

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dude. This was amazing.
He starts off as a boring, edgy Mary Sue character, a narcissist you don't really like… Then BAM! Everything's corrupt! The dialogue changes! It breaks the fourth wall! It's outstanding! The corrupted page that changed each refresh threw me for a loop! I'm very impressed!

It thinks it's real, but since the foundation isn't real, then neither is it and it KNOWS this and it makes you feel kind of bad for it.

Take your upvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why does the +1 button only work once?

If i press it enough they will let the hero go home right? ….right?

how many +1's do they need I'll keep pressing if that's what they want.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wasn't feeling this until the 3rd offset, when it suddenly became chilling, heartbreaking and suprisingly subtle to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In my small little headcanon, 2786 speedran Amnesia almost like the WR did.

I know that's not what it said in the article, but… it makes more sense he walked through walls to get to the end, than pulled a gun out of nowhere.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What happens to 2786? Did the foundation manage to contain it-or did they simply just fuck off and it ceases it's existence? I'm really curious about this bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Real nitpicky, I know, but the film strip doesn't summon the werewolf in Cabin in the Woods. According to the director, the film strip summons Kevin from We Need to Talk About Kevin. The Amulet is the thing that summons the werewolf.

https://thecabininthewoods.wikia.com/wiki/Monster_Items#cite_note-io9-0

item.title: Re: Format on Surface Pro?
item.content:encoded: Works on Surface 2, perhaps just give it extra time to load!

item.title: Format on Surface Pro?
item.content:encoded: I know there is a button I need to click to get the other iterations of the file, but it doesn't appear in any browser on the Surface Pro, any advice?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My favourite element of this story is the subtle 4th wall breaking of this SCP. He/She/It ONLY appears in story works, particularly fictional and fantasy ;) yet he appears in person in the foundation.

item.title: I made an audio adaptation of this!
item.content:encoded: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NoJb9u-c1vc - This SCP is criminally under-rated. I really enjoyed reading it, thank you.
Holy crap! Thanks everyone

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I clicked random SCP and I'm so glad it brought me to this. This is, by far, my favorite SCP that I have ever read, I enjoyed the way it was done, what with the re-calibration and whatnot, very different from the other SCPs, which is something I look for. And this has truly exceeded my expectations, well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel rather bad for this anomaly. It wanted to do the right thing and ended up getting caught in it's own personal hell.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: WIP

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Making the loop stop is a simple fix. My thought what that it would bamboozle the reader for a hot second before realizing what was happening

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought it was done, so I hit "Recalibrate" to see what would happen. Then I upvoted.
My only problem: If you hit the button enough, the article loops through iterations, rather than stopping at the final one.
Final thing: I would love to see 2786 vs 3143.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Brilliant. Great job.
I wish there were more recalibrations, though…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I want to add more, but i am trying to think of what to add! There was another one in there that was rather superfluous, i need something unique.

I was considering putting a small pseudotale about it going on one of its trademarked adventures, except it is revealed at the end to be just another narrative scenario the foundation created for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm up voting mostly because this hooked me and it didn't let go. This is a really nice use of this format screw. However, I'm in agreement with shaggy that this could use a couple more iterations.

item.title: Re: awesome concept
item.content:encoded: Thanks! I really like incorporating my code into my work (So long as the content itself can hold its own without it!)

item.title: awesome concept
item.content:encoded: nothing much to say about this one, just that the pictures are unsettling, but in a good way. keep up with the good work. enjoyed 3959 btw

item.title: Re: I think the 4th wall just shattered
item.content:encoded:
You just made my day, I needed that. Thanks.

Honestly 2719 will prolly be my favorite abstract concept SCP, but I'm really glad everyone likes this one :)

item.title: I think the 4th wall just shattered
item.content:encoded:
Special Containment Procedures: SCP-2786 does not physically exist and therefore no containment measures are necessary.

Oh I get it, The Foundation does not exist and the anomaly effects horror fiction, therefore it showed up despite not existing because the Foundation universe is a work of fiction and co-nonexists with the entity. This is by far the most original and abstract concept I have seen yet and it was very entertaining to read.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
whoops, forgot to link them

thanks for that

edit: I'm gonna figure out a way to get you to switch to no vote, mark my words. More corrupted divs or something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Heads up, you're going to actually have to cite the actual sources to be compliant, not just say where they're from.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Both pictures were actually taken as-is from Flickr's awesome creative commons search.

If you had the base images, both could be made pretty easily in something like Paint.NET.

For the first one, you just need to add a second layer for the figure, then reduce the opacity so it's partially see through.

The second one is obviously monochrome'd, then probably the figure's outline was selected and possibly made into its own layer. Then they probably used a soften effect, followed by a defocus or gaussian blur, both only on the person so the background remains clear.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The pictures used in this are really good. They give off both an unsettling feeling and apprehension. What photo editing software did you use?
+1 from me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Whatever I did with the coding" was a pain, I basically made a webpage in a webpage, there is very little actual wikidot formatting going on, just the header. Worth it though

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eloquent response shaggy!

Let me clear some things up.

  • I'd love to add more offset divs, i just didn't want it to seem redundant.
  • collquially is correct, informal, among friends On second thought, it more applies to candid conversation than all interactions. Fixed
  • it affects its own documentation so the first iteration is already warped, the events of the document happen before the reader accesses it
  • in the oblique page, the skip almost realizes it is not in the correct narrative, which may allow it some freedom to escape, so the system overrides that and pushes him into a new narrative. I wanted to add more, I just don't know what, exactly.

Thanks for the wonderful feedback

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, this is great! It fixes all the issues I had with the earlier version, both formatting and narrative-wise. Whatever you did with the new coding works like a charm. And while the skip is still able to alter both reality and fiction, they can't usefully distinguish the two in any context. The Foundation is able to exploit this aspect of their perception, as well as their egotistical self-image, to successfully contain them.

A hearty +1 for you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
nitpicks

+ show block
– hide block

███████ ████.

It's a little silly blackboxing this with all the references already in the article.

do not seem fazed by its presence and react colloquially.

I think you mean to say "candidly".

Anyways, the jump-scare was neat, and I like the meta aspect of the looping page, and a lot of what's going on here, but I do feel like it's cut a bit too short. I think this could have used another iteration or two to thread everything together in a more satisfying manner, as right now the whole conflict is mostly glossed over, giving me the sense that I'm rushing through the beats that could use a bit more breathing room.

more deets:

+ show block
– hide block

So, for the first version, "The affected copy was played on a loop until its current containment measures were devised. SCP-2786 has been placed in its enhanced narrative complex and containment is considered a success." I'm guessing this is referring to the entire format itself? If so, I'm wondering how it initially begins to affect its documentation (in the subsequent iteration). Seems like it 'just does'.

But alright, it does. It gets loose, screws around a bit, and messes up its file. I kinda feel like there could be a bit more at this point, because it pretty much goes from 0 to 10 in the span of a dozen words, so by the time we get to the following log, the tonal shift is a touch jarring. If not another iteration between the corrupted doc vs the Oblique doc, then I think expanding the corrupted document to sort of extend its time in that trapped state would help to flow more seamlessly into its explosion immediately afterwards.

Next up, I really feel like I'm missing something in the Oblique iteration. I'm not entirely sure what's supposed to be happening at the end, how the anomaly is being counteracted. It seems like it just cuts off because of a loss of direction, so the next scene could just get started. All ears if I'm overlooking something.

Final iteration, and the end of the description in particular is pretty solid for an endpoint. My only real complaint here is that I think that I should be feeling something for the character, but I just don't. This may be due to me not really having enough time to get emotionally invested in the character prior to this point, primarily because the conflict feels undersold at the moment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
you're cool

thanks buddy :)

also enjoy your theater weekend thing

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is just cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I loved it, just had to put it in there, and I had to make him infuriating. Honestly these are all some of my favorite horror stuff. I'll carry my copy of House of Leaves with me until I die. Most SCP-like book I've ever read, too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Although I liked the previous one, I like this one much better.
Though I must admit, the way it handled Amnesia: The Dark Descent made me so mad, since it's one of my favourite games :P

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You can click it up to 5 times before it loops!

And thanks, I definitely wanted to make him look like a good guy gone bad, which is sorta the opposite of Murphy Law, who generally seems shady but has otherwise positive intentions more or less

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Got it. Didn't realize I needed to click the button twice.

If your goal was to set them apart, consider it successfully achieved. They're definitely different characters. I would not turn my back on 2786.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ambiguity, I suppose, I didn't want it to be too on the nose in the interview. The original article didn't have the table, maybe I'll change it though. Good point!

edit: Maybe I'll add a footnote saying they had to redact the name for copyright reasons xD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Out of curiosity, why censor Crystal Lake but not any of the other horror settings?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
blushes

Thanks :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And this is why I upvote. I never thought this was possible before, but you've proven me wrong. And it's beautiful. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It works fine on mobile for me my friend. The button might take a second to pop up though.

What are you running? I might have to check cross platform. The entire article is written in html/css instead of wikidot formatting, so it might have adverse effects when viewed from other browsers, not sure though!

And as much as I love Murphy Law, I really wanted to set them apart in terms of MO, personality and such. It would have been too easy to draw directly from that masterpiece.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think I got most of this through page source, but just in case - is there any way to view this on a mobile device? If the answer is no, I promise not to whine.

That said, I found myself imagining this SCP and Murphy Law having a drink in a seedy dive bar, trying to suss each other out. Well Bourbon for Law, double vodka for 2786 (because it mixes with everything).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Do you think I should move the table to draw out the reveal?

If so, where would I move it to?

End of document or a sixth page perhaps?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Edgy tryhard is his character

That's why its the Archetype, its your patented narrative mary sue come to life.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the gimmick and the concept is solid, but the way you wrote some of this comes off as borderline cringy. Take this for instance:

I am in control now. You thought you could hold me down? You thought you were the heroes of this story? Think again. I'm the hero of this story, my story.
This will always be my story.

Maybe it's just me, but this just feels like "tryhard edgy evil character".

Novote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed with djkaktus. This works a lot better, the only thing being that I don't get some creepy ones before I get to the actual article which makes the reveal have no fridge horror effect like it had when I reviewed the draft. Just one or two short creepy unexplained ones before the real one would be nice… but I digress. This works as is. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! Hopefully it is long enough now. Didn't want to bog it down too much because you're still clicking through five pages. okay 10 pages

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah this was a better way to do this.
